title: shivparvatia ciliolata
intro: Shivparvatia ciliolata, commonly known as Himalayan grapevine, is a perennial climber that belongs to the family Lardizabalaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties and is used to treat various health conditions. Shivparvatia ciliolata has been reported to possess several health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hepatoprotective properties, which can help alleviate liver damage, reduce oxidative stress, and combat age-related disorders. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic actions, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anticancer activities.
benefits: Shivparvatia ciliolata has many benefits, such as being a potential anti-inflammatory agent, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, gout, and other inflammatory diseases. Its antioxidant properties can protect against cell damage, reducing the risk of cancer, neurodegenerative disorders, and cardiovascular diseases. Additionally, Shivparvatia ciliolata has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities, making it a promising treatment for infections and wound healing. By harnessing these benefits, individuals can improve their overall health, enhance their immune system, and lead a longer, healthier life with reduced risk of chronic diseases.
constituents: Shivparvatia ciliolata has many active constituents, such as flavonoids like quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce the risk of chronic diseases like heart disease and cancer. The plant also contains alkaloids like shivparvatine and ciliolate, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities, making them useful in the treatment of infections and respiratory disorders. Additionally, Shivparvatia ciliolata contains terpenoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory, anti-cancer, and immunomodulatory effects, improving overall health and well-being. The medicinal constituents of Shivparvatia ciliolata have the potential to improve people's daily lives by reducing the risk of chronic diseases, treating infections, and promoting overall health and well-being.
parts: Shivparvatia ciliolata has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, roots, and aerial parts, which are used in traditional medicine. The leaves of Shivparvatia ciliolata are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The roots of the plant contain alkaloids and glycosides, which have been found to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory activities. The aerial parts of Shivparvatia ciliolata contain saponins and phytosterols, which are believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
side_effects: Shivparvatia ciliolata can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing dizziness and nausea due to its emetic properties, which can lead to vomiting. In some cases, excessive consumption of the plant's extracts can result in allergic reactions, including skin rashes and respiratory issues. Improper use of Shivparvatia ciliolata can also lead to liver damage and kidney problems, particularly if taken in large quantities or combined with other medications. Additionally, its high content of volatile oils can cause headaches, fatigue, and stomach discomfort in individuals who are sensitive to its compounds.
